discovery.text: Recommended via Concierge Chat for Boston late-summer trip

narrative.summary: Converted granite warehouse on Boston's waterfront with exposed brick and post-and-beam character. Steps from the ferry terminals, Faneuil Hall, and the North End. Best for harbour energy and water access.

blocks.title: The Place
blocks.body: A 19th-century granite merchant warehouse converted into a boutique hotel with exposed brick walls, post-and-beam ceilings, and rooms that feel genuinely historic rather than faux-historic. It's a smaller property, which means it feels personal rather than transactional. The State Street location puts you at the edge of the Financial District and steps from the waterfront.

blocks.title: Location
blocks.body: Right on State Street, minutes from the harbour ferry to Charlestown or the Harbor Islands. Quincy Market, Faneuil Hall, and the North End are all a short walk. This is maximum Boston waterfront — good for harbour cruises, lobster rolls, and the Freedom Trail.

blocks.title: Go When
blocks.body: Late August for Boston Harborfest and the summer harbor cruise season. The waterfront is most alive mid-summer and the ferry routes to the Harbor Islands are worth the trip.

_hotel.bestFor: Water lovers, harbour atmosphere, historic character
